meta.title: Compress Video to 25 MB Online — Free, No Upload | {{SITE_NAME}}
meta.description: Shrink any video to under 25 MB for Discord Nitro Basic, Gmail and Outlook attachments. Runs in your browser — your video is never uploaded.
og.title: Compress Video to 25 MB — Free, No Upload
og.description: Fit any video under 25 MB in your browser. Nothing is uploaded.
hero.title: Compress a video to 25 MB
hero.lead: 25 MB is the magic number for Gmail and Outlook attachments and Discord's classic Nitro upload limit. Drop your video below and it will be compressed to just under 25 MB on your own device — no upload, no account, no watermark.
prose.h2: Why 25 MB, and what to expect
prose.p1: Gmail and Outlook both cap attachments at 25 MB, and Discord's Nitro Basic tier uses the same limit. Most services check the exact byte count, so a "25.1 MB" file bounces. This tool always lands under the limit: it works out exactly how much to compress so the video fits, leaves room for the audio, then checks the result and compresses again if the first pass came out a touch too big. You'll typically get a file between 22 and 25 MB — never over.
prose.p2: What quality fits in 25 MB depends entirely on length. A 30-second clip stays crisp 1080p. A 5-minute video lands around 720p and still looks good on a phone. A 20-minute screen recording will be visibly compressed — at some point the tool lowers resolution and frame rate rather than smearing every frame. Want to know before you start? The bitrate calculator shows the exact video bitrate and best resolution 25 MB gives your clip. The compression runs locally in your browser, so a 300 MB source doesn't need a 300 MB upload before work even starts.
faq.q1: Will the file really be under 25 MB?
faq.a1: Yes. The tool checks the final size and automatically compresses a little more if needed. It never hands you a file over the target.
faq.q2: Is the video uploaded to a server?
faq.a2: No. Compression happens in your browser on your own device. There is no upload and no server-side processing — which is also why it's fast and free.
faq.q3: Why does Gmail still reject my 25 MB file?
faq.a3: Email encodes attachments in a format that adds roughly 33% overhead, so Gmail's effective limit for attachments is lower than 25 MB of file. If your compressed file is close to 25 MB and still bounces, compress to a custom target of 18 MB on the generic compressor instead.
faq.a4: On a typical laptop in Chrome, faster than the video's own duration — a 4-minute video compresses in under 4 minutes, usually much less, because it uses your device's own video hardware.
faq.q5: Can I pick a different size?
faq.a5: Yes — use the full compressor, which has 8, 10, 16, 25 and 50 MB presets plus a custom field.
